proposed_title: Visa Chargeback Guide for Ecommerce Merchants
summary: Build a flagship merchant guide to Visa dispute concepts with an ecommerce lens. Focus on reason-code patterns, evidence categories, deadlines, and prevention actions.

proposed_title: What Merchants Need to Know About Chargeback Time Limits by Card Network
target_keyword: chargeback time limits by card network
summary: Explain how dispute windows vary by network and why merchants should not rely on one generic deadline assumption. Focus on planning and operational risk.

proposed_title: Questions to Ask a Chargeback Vendor Before You Install
summary: A buyer-enablement checklist covering implementation, support, evidence quality, auditability, automation, and fees. The goal is better vendor conversations and fewer surprises.
Position dispute tooling around the evidence needs of digital sellers. Cover access logs, entitlement data, cancellation evidence, and why generic physical-goods workflows can be a poor fit.
Help recurring-revenue merchants evaluate tooling based on consent evidence, renewal history, reminder support, and cancellation workflows.

proposed_title: Chargeback Dashboard Metrics Every Merchant Tool Should Show
summary: Help buyers evaluate dashboards based on operational usefulness, not pretty charts. Cover trend views, reason-code breakdowns, recovery tracking, and actionability.
Give teams a reusable format for analyzing why a dispute happened and what to change. Include fields for reason code, product, policy gap, communication failure, and owner.

proposed_title: Chargebacks on Same-Day Delivery and Local Courier Orders
summary: Explain how merchants using local couriers can defend disputes when traditional carrier tracking is limited. Cover proof of handoff, delivery photos, and customer confirmations.

proposed_title: Chargebacks on Bundle, Kit, and Multi-Item Orders
summary: Help merchants selling bundles or kits prove what was ordered and delivered. Cover SKU mapping, product-page screenshots, packing evidence, and partial-order disputes.

proposed_title: Chargebacks on Service or Consultation Orders: What Evidence Helps?
summary: Tailor guidance to merchants selling appointments, consulting, or service bundles through Shopify. Cover booking logs, attendance records, acceptance proofs, and scope documentation.

proposed_title: Chargebacks on Electronics Orders: Serial Numbers, Activation, and Proof
summary: Show electronics sellers how serial numbers, activation, setup, and warranty data can support better cases. Keep it practical for DTC and omnichannel teams.

proposed_title: Chargeback Operations for Peak Season: Staffing, SLAs, and Triage
summary: A planning guide for merchants entering high-volume periods. Cover staffing, workflow design, triage rules, evidence templates, and QA under pressure.

proposed_title: How to Migrate Off a Chargeback Vendor Without Losing Case History
summary: A migration checklist for merchants changing providers or moving from manual processes into software. Cover exports, workflow continuity, evidence history, and reporting continuity.

proposed_title: How to Preserve Evidence Before a Dispute Leaves the Payment Rails
summary: A cross-functional checklist for preserving records early when a payment issue may evolve into a bigger dispute. Cover order data, policy versions, carrier records, and communication logs.

proposed_title: How to Write an RFP for Chargeback Software
summary: Provide an RFP structure for merchants evaluating dispute platforms. Include requirements around integrations, evidence workflow, analytics, automation controls, and pricing disclosure.
